chapter_context.summary: Describes the punishment of Leviathan, the preservation of the fruitful vineyard of the Lord, the purging of Jacob's iniquity, and the great trumpet assembly.

outline.text: Punishment of Leviathan, the Crooked Serpent
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Punishment of Leviathan, the Crooked Serpent (27:1), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Song of the Fruitful Vineyard of the Lord
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Song of the Fruitful Vineyard of the Lord (27:2-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Purging Jacob's Idolatry and Assembly of Outcasts
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Purging Jacob's Idolatry and Assembly of Outcasts (27:7-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 27, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
